(2) the report_ast facet-wiring: is _facet_contract() now using research_contract.compile_contract (the fix), and does report_ast import cleanly? (3) the exact score_report_race.py invocation — read its __main__/docstring: it needs the DRB task prompt/reference/criteria (data/prompt_data/query.jsonl) and judges with gpt-5.5 via OpenRouter; what are the required args and env? (4) the "2572 refused corroborators / 279 units" — does the composer need corroborator bindings, or does it compose fine from the 5294 admitted primary bindings? (5) provenance_graph.json — does it exist and strict-load (the composer re-verifies bindings against it)? Produce the EXACT command sequence + env for a clean compose->score run someone could execute directly. Do NOT modify anything.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). ===score_report_race.py FULL===
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Score a composed POLARIS report with RACE (DeepResearch Bench) against a benchmark task.

RACE (third_party/deep_research_bench/deepresearch_bench_race.py) is REFERENCE-BASED: it matches
the target article, the reference article, and the per-task criteria by EXACT prompt string, then
an LLM judge (openai/gpt-5.5 via OpenRouter) scores both target and reference on the task's
dynamic-weighted dimensions. Overall = target/(target+reference) (0.5 == parity with reference).

This wrapper: (1) reads a report.md, (2) writes it as the target jsonl under the benchmark task's
EXACT prompt (so ref+criteria align), (3) invokes the official RACE harness limited to that task,
(4) prints results/race/<model>/race_result.txt.

Usage:
    set -a && . ./.env && set +a
    python scripts/score_report_race.py --report outputs/live_compose/run2/report.md \
        --task-id 72 --model-name polaris_task72
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
DRB = ROOT / "third_party" / "deep_research_bench"


def main() -> int:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    ap.add_argument("--report", required=True, help="path to report.md")
    ap.add_argument("--task-id", default="72")
    ap.add_argument("--model-name", default="polaris_task72")
    ap.add_argument("--race-model", default=os.getenv("RACE_MODEL", "openai/gpt-5.5"))
    ap.add_argument("--max-workers", type=int, default=4)
    args = ap.parse_args()

    if not os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"):
        print("BLOCKED: OPENROUTER_API_KEY not set — source .env first", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    report_text = Path(args.report).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    # Find the benchmark task's EXACT prompt (target/ref/criteria all key on it).
    task = None
    for line in (DRB / "data/prompt_data/query.jsonl").read_text().splitlines():
        o = json.loads(line)
        if str(o["id"]) == str(args.task_id):
            task = o
            break
    if task is None:
        print(f"BLOCKED: task id {args.task_id} not in query.jsonl", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    prompt = task["prompt"]
    lang = task.get("language", "en")

    # Single-task query file so ONLY this task is scored.
    q_path = DRB / f"data/prompt_data/query_task{args.task_id}.jsonl"
    q_path.write_text(json.dumps(task, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

    # Target article jsonl (raw). Article carries the benchmark prompt so RACE aligns it with the
    # reference+criteria. This is an apples-to-apples score ONLY when the report was composed to
    # answer THIS prompt.
    target = {"id": task["id"], "prompt": prompt, "article": report_text}
    raw_dir = DRB / "data/test_data/raw_data"
    (raw_dir / f"{args.model_name}.jsonl").write_text(
        json.dumps(target, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"[race] target={args.model_name}.jsonl  article_chars={len(report_text)}  "
          f"prompt_task={args.task_id} lang={lang}")

    out_dir = DRB / f"results/race/{args.model_name}"
    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    env = dict(os.environ)
    env["LLM_BACKEND"] = "openrouter"
    env["RACE_MODEL"] = args.race_model
    lang_flag = "--only_en" if lang == "en" else "--only_zh"
    cmd = [
        sys.executable, "-u", "deepresearch_bench_race.py", args.model_name,
        "--raw_data_dir", "data/test_data/raw_data",
        "--cleaned_data_dir", "data/test_data/cleaned_data",
        "--query_file", str(q_path.relative_to(DRB)),
        "--output_dir", str(out_dir.relative_to(DRB)),
        "--max_workers", str(args.max_workers),
        lang_flag, "--force",
    ]
    print("[race] running:", " ".join(cmd))
    r = subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=str(DRB), env=env)
    res_file = out_dir / "race_result.txt"
    if res_file.exists():
        print("\n===== RACE RESULT =====")
        print(res_file.read_text())
    else:
        print("[race] NO race_result.txt produced (see harness log above)", file=sys.stderr)
    return r.returncode


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

153	def jparse(s: str):
154	    """Parse the JSON payload of an LLM reply. It may be an ARRAY (the composer's node lists) or an
155	    OBJECT (research_contract's compile prompt returns `{...}`), with or without a ```json fence and
156	    with or without prose around it.
157	
158	    THE BUG THIS FIXES. The old body was `re.search(r'\\[.*\\]', s, re.S)` — it extracted a JSON
159	    *array* and nothing else. On the compile prompt's *object* reply the regex grabbed from the first
160	    inner array to the last inner array and json.loads raised "Extra data", so compile_contract
161	    (research_contract.py:456) crashed or silently fell back to the regex-floor contract on any
162	    genuinely new question. The model was never at fault; this parser was.
163	
164	    It stays array-safe: we decode the FIRST top-level container we find, so a reply that IS an array
165	    of objects still parses as an array (the earliest opener is `[`), exactly as before.
166	    """
167	    s = re.sub(r'^```(?:json)?|```$', '', (s or '').strip(), flags=re.M).strip()
168	    if not s:
169	        return None
170	    try:
171	        return json.loads(s)                      # the common case: the reply IS the JSON
172	    except Exception:
173	        pass
174	    dec = json.JSONDecoder()
175	    for i, ch in enumerate(s):                    # first `{` or `[`, whichever comes first
176	        if ch in '{[':
177	            try:
178	                val, _end = dec.raw_decode(s, i)  # balanced scan: trailing prose can no longer poison it
179	                return val
180	            except ValueError:
181	                continue
182	    return None

192	                policy_name: str = 'journal_articles_only',
193	                expect_cards_sha: str = '', expect_graph_sha: str = '') -> CardBundle:
194	    """ONE explicit card-bundle path, plus ITS graph and ledger HASHES. No defaults that point at a file
195	    somebody else is writing.
196	
197	    A hash mismatch is a REFUSAL, not a warning. The composer that ran before this one read a card file
198	    whose provenance it never established, and the number it produced (0.4603) is not a real number.
199	    """
200	    cards_sha, graph_sha = sha256_file(cards_path), sha256_file(graph_path)
201	    ledger_sha = sha256_file(ledger_path) if Path(ledger_path).exists() else ''
202	    if expect_cards_sha and expect_cards_sha != cards_sha:
203	        raise SystemExit(f'CARD BUNDLE IS NOT THE ONE PINNED: expected {expect_cards_sha[:16]}…, '
204	                         f'{cards_path} is {cards_sha[:16]}…')
205	    if expect_graph_sha and expect_graph_sha != graph_sha:
206	        raise SystemExit(f'GRAPH IS NOT THE ONE PINNED: expected {expect_graph_sha[:16]}…, '
207	                         f'{graph_path} is {graph_sha[:16]}…')
208	    graph = P.Graph.from_json(json.loads(Path(graph_path).read_text()))   # STRICT LOAD. May raise.
209	    cards = json.loads(Path(cards_path).read_text())

211	                      graph_sha=graph_sha, ledger_sha=ledger_sha)

214	def reverify(b: CardBundle) -> list[str]:
215	    """BEFORE ANY LLM CALL. Every PRIMARY and every CORROBORATING binding, re-resolved from the bytes.
216	
217	    Not "on the way out". Not "in a preflight somebody may run". HERE, before a single token is spent,
218	    because a card that cannot prove its bytes must never reach a writer's context window — the writer
219	    will faithfully report it, and everything downstream will then be checking a fabrication against
220	    itself.
221	    """
222	    ok, bad = [], []
223	    # ITERATE A SNAPSHOT. The loop body ADDS corroborating cards to `b.cards`, and iterating a dict
224	    # while inserting into it is a RuntimeError. It does not fire today only because no card in this
225	    # bundle carries a corroborating source — i.e. the crash is waiting for the first one that does,
226	    # which is precisely the code path we least want to discover in production.
227	    for cid in list(b.cards):
228	        r = b.resolve(cid)
229	        (ok if r.ok else bad).append(cid if r.ok else f'{cid}: {r.refusal}')
230	        # A CORROBORATING SOURCE IS A CITATION. It gets the same chain, independently — it is exactly as
231	        # capable of naming a document its span never came from as the primary is.
232	        for j, cs in enumerate(b.cards[cid].get('corroborating_sources') or []):
233	            sub = dict(cs)
234	            sub.setdefault('id', f'{cid}#corr{j}')
235	            if sub['id'] not in b.cards:
236	                b.cards[sub['id']] = sub
237	            rc = b.resolve(sub['id'])
238	            if not rc.ok:
239	                bad.append(f'{cid} corroborating[{j}]: {rc.refusal}')
240	    return bad

245	def _select(b: CardBundle, sub: str, k: int = 12) -> list[str]:
246	    """Pick the ADMITTED cards most relevant to this subsection. An unbound card is not a candidate."""
247	    want = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', sub.lower())}
248	    scored = []
249	    for cid in b.admitted_ids():
250	        c = b.cards[cid]
251	        blob = (f"{c.get('claim','')} {c.get('level','')} {c.get('method','')} "
252	                f"{' '.join(c.get('mechanisms') or [])} {' '.join(c.get('facet_tags') or [])} "
253	                f"{c.get('outcome','')} {c.get('technology','')} {c.get('industry','')}").lower()
254	        have = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', blob)}
255	        scored.append((len(want & have), cid))
256	    scored.sort(key=lambda x: -x[0])
257	    return [cid for s, cid in scored[:k] if s > 0] or [cid for _, cid in scored[:5]]

260	def _fmt_cards(b: CardBundle, card_ids: list[str]) -> str:
261	    """THE WRITER SEES THE SPAN AND THE CARD ID. IT NEVER SEES AN ATTRIBUTION.
262	
263	    REFUSES an unbound card — it does not skip it, it raises. A composer that silently drops the cards it
264	    cannot justify still produces a document, and the document it produces is the one that gets
265	    published.
266	    """
267	    out = []
268	    for cid in card_ids:
269	        r = b.resolve(cid)
270	        if not r.ok:
271	            raise ValueError(f'_fmt_cards REFUSES an unbound card: {cid} — {r.refusal}')
272	        c = r.card
273	        span = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', r.span).strip()
274	        mech = (f" | mechanism STATED BY THE PAPER: {', '.join(c['mechanisms'])}"
275	                if c.get('mechanisms') else '')
276	        out.append(
277	            f"card_id: {cid}\n"
278	            f"  THE SOURCE SAYS (verbatim — every number you write MUST appear here): \"{span}\"\n"
279	            f"  unit of analysis: {c.get('level') or c.get('unit_of_analysis') or '?'} | "
280	            f"horizon: {c.get('horizon') or '?'} | method: {c.get('method') or c.get('design') or '?'}"
281	            f"{mech}")
282	    return '\n'.join(out)

294	{cards}

335	5. Open each paragraph with a claim, not a topic announcement. Never mention this pipeline or "the

337	6. ** SAY EACH FACT ONCE. ** A finding stated in full in one place and REFERRED BACK TO in another is a
338	   review; a finding re-narrated in eight places is a list. Where a fact below is marked SPENT or is
339	   licensed only in a NEW ROLE, obey that: the sentence that restates it is deleted, and you will have
340	   spent your paragraph on nothing.
341	
342	Return ONLY the JSON array."""

348	    """Parse the model's structured output into TYPED NODES. Anything malformed is DROPPED, with a

265	class CardBundle:
266	    """ONE card lane. ONE graph. ONE policy. THE SEAM IS GONE.
267	
268	    There were TWO DISCONNECTED CARD LANES: the miner wrote `evidence_cards_v2.json` and the composer
269	    read `evidence_cards.json` — a file whose cards carry no manifestation, no hash and no expression,
270	    and whose four Frey & Osborne cards were mined out of an ORA LANDING PAGE. The composer could not
271	    have detected that, because the lane it read had nothing in it to detect with.
272	
273	    A bundle is constructed from an EXPLICIT path plus the graph, and it REFUSES to exist if the bytes
274	    on disk are not the bytes it was promised.
275	    """
276	
277	    def __init__(self, cards: list[dict], graph: P.Graph,
278	                 policy: P.SourcePolicy = P.JOURNAL_ONLY,
279	                 cards_sha: str = '', graph_sha: str = '', ledger_sha: str = ''):
280	        self.graph = graph
281	        self.policy = policy
282	        self.cards_sha = cards_sha
283	        self.graph_sha = graph_sha
284	        self.ledger_sha = ledger_sha
285	        # A DUPLICATE CARD ID IS A REFUSAL, NOT AN OVERWRITE.
286	        #
287	        # This dict used to be filled with `self.cards[cid] = c`, and the miner's id is
288	        # `{doi}:{span_start}-{span_end}` — so TWO FINDINGS MINED FROM ONE SPAN COLLIDE. On the first
289	        # real bundle, 13 of 52 cards were silently overwritten: the composer printed "52 cards, 0
290	        # refused" and had 39. The evidence did not fail a check. IT LEFT WITHOUT ONE, which is the
291	        # quieter half of every defect in this file's header. And a sentence citing a colliding id
292	        # names an AMBIGUOUS card: same span, different claim, different act, different table row.
293	        self.cards: dict[str, dict] = {}
294	        dupes: dict[str, int] = {}
295	        for c in cards:
296	            cid = c.get('id')
297	            if not cid:
298	                raise ValueError('a card with no `id` cannot be cited by a sentence, and is REFUSED')
299	            if cid in self.cards:
300	                dupes[cid] = dupes.get(cid, 1) + 1
301	                continue
302	            self.cards[cid] = c
303	        if dupes:
304	            n = sum(dupes.values()) - len(dupes)
305	            raise ValueError(
306	                f'{len(dupes)} DUPLICATE CARD ID(S) ({n} cards would be silently dropped) — a bundle '
307	                f'whose ids are ambiguous is not a bundle, and a sentence citing one of them names no '
308	                f'determinate evidence. Offenders: {sorted(dupes)[:3]}')
309	        self._cache: dict[str, Resolution] = {}
310	        # Every surname AND venue the bundle knows. Used ONLY NEGATIVELY — to refuse a source name in a
311	        # lane that is not allowed to have one. We never infer WHICH source from a surname again.
312	        #
313	        # THE OLD SET ADDED AUTHORS ONLY, AND ONLY AT len>=4. Sol walked through both holes: a journal
314	        # name ("Science reports that ...") was never in the set, so it shipped under an AER card; and a
315	        # sub-4-char surname ("Wu", "Ng") was invisible, as was a bare surname when the author was stored
316	        # as a full name ("Daron Acemoglu" is one token to `\bdaron acemoglu\b`, so "Acemoglu" alone slips
317	        # the exact match). We now index venues too, and index each NAME TOKEN, not only the whole string.
318	        self._source_words: set[str] = set()      # single tokens, matched at word boundaries
319	        self._source_phrases: set[str] = set()    # multi-word venue names, matched as a phrase
320	        for c in self.cards.values():
321	            for a in (c.get('authors') or []):
322	                self._index_person(a)
323	            self._index_venue(c.get('venue'))
324	        for w in graph.works.values():
325	            for a in (w.authors or []):
326	                self._index_person(a)
327	            self._index_venue(getattr(w, 'venue', None))
328	
329	    def _index_person(self, name: str) -> None:
330	        """A person can be named by the whole string OR by a single token of it (the surname alone)."""
331	        n = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', (name or '').strip().lower())
332	        if not n:
333	            return
334	        toks = re.findall(r"[a-z][a-z.'’\-]*[a-z]", n)  # drop bare initials ("j."), keep "wu", "ng"
335	        if len(n) >= 4:
336	            self._source_words.add(n)
337	        for t in toks:
338	            if len(t) >= 2:
339	                self._source_words.add(t)
340	
341	    def _index_venue(self, venue: str) -> None:
342	        """A venue can be named whole ("the American Economic Review") or, when it is one distinctive
343	        word ("Science", "Nature", "Technovation"), by that word. We do NOT add single tokens of a
344	        multi-word venue — "economic" or "review" are common words and would refuse honest prose."""
345	        v = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', (venue or '').strip().lower())
346	        if not v:
347	            return
348	        core = re.sub(r'^the\s+', '', v)
349	        toks = core.split(' ')
350	        if len(toks) == 1 and len(toks[0]) >= 4:
351	            self._source_words.add(toks[0])
352	        else:
353	            self._source_phrases.add(v)
354	            if core != v:
355	                self._source_phrases.add(core)
356

358	    def resolve(self, card_id: str) -> Resolution:
359	        """sentence -> card -> bound span -> manifestation_id + content_hash -> permitted expression
360	        -> attribution.
361	
362	        EVERY step is re-derived from the graph HERE, at use time. Nothing stored on the card is
363	        trusted: not the attribution, not the permitted set, not the expression, not the hash.
364	
365	        `verify_span()` is called FIRST — before any field of the card is read for any purpose — because
366	        a card whose bytes do not verify is not a weak card, it is NOT A CARD, and reading its `venue`
367	        to make a decision is how a landing page became a peer-reviewed article.
368	        """
369	        if card_id in self._cache:
370	            return self._cache[card_id]
371	        r = self._resolve(card_id)
372	        self._cache[card_id] = r
373	        return r

375	    def _resolve(self, card_id: str) -> Resolution:
376	        c = self.cards.get(card_id)
377	        if c is None:
378	            return Resolution(False, card_id, refusal='NO_SUCH_CARD_IN_BUNDLE')
379	
380	        binding = _binding_from_card(c)
381	        if not binding:
382	            return Resolution(False, card_id, refusal='CARD_IS_UNBOUND (no manifestation_id/content_hash '
383	                                                      '/span offsets — a DOI names a work, and a work has '
384	                                                      'no bytes)')
385	        # ---- 1. THE BYTES. Untrusted input; re-check offsets, hash, and the text itself.
386	        if not self.graph.verify_span(binding):
387	            return Resolution(False, card_id, refusal=f'SPAN_DOES_NOT_VERIFY against '
388	                                                      f'{binding["manifestation_id"]}')
389	        mid = binding['manifestation_id']
390	        m = self.graph.manifestations[mid]
391	
392	        # ---- 2. THE POLICY. What, if anything, may THIS SPAN name?
393	        #         Sol V9 §4: the question is asked about the BINDING, not about the manifestation. A
394	        #         manifestation-wide answer would hand THIS span whatever permission any OTHER span in
395	        #         the same document had earned — and the spans peer review rewrote sit in the very same
396	        #         document as the spans it left alone. Permission is per span or it is fiction.
397	        att = self.graph.resolve_attribution(binding, self.policy)
398	        if not att.admitted:
399	            return Resolution(False, card_id, refusal=f'SOURCE_POLICY_REFUSES: {att.refusal}')
400	
401	        # ---- 3. THE CARD'S OWN STORED TARGET MUST BE THE ONE THE GRAPH RESOLVES *NOW*.
402	        #         A stored target is a cache, and a cache that is not checked is how a stale permission
403	        #         keeps naming a journal it was never allowed to name.
404	        stored = c.get('attribution_target_expression_id')
405	        if stored and stored != att.names_expression_id:
406	            return Resolution(False, card_id,
407	                              refusal=f'STALE_ATTRIBUTION_TARGET: card says {stored!r}, the graph '
408	                                      f'resolves {att.names_expression_id!r} under `{self.policy.name}`')
409	
410	        # ---- 4. THE ATTRIBUTION IS RENDERED FROM THE GRAPH. The card's own string is never read.
411	        expr = self.graph.expressions[att.names_expression_id]
412	        work = self.graph.works[expr.work_id]
413	        prose = render_attribution(work, expr)
414	        if not prose:
415	            return Resolution(False, card_id,
416	                              refusal=f'EXPRESSION_KIND_IS_NOT_RENDERABLE_AS_A_CITATION: {expr.kind!r}')
417	
418	        return Resolution(
419	            True, card_id, manifestation_id=mid, content_hash=binding['content_hash'],
420	            expression_id=binding.get('expression_id') or m.expression_id,
421	            names_expression_id=att.names_expression_id,
422	            span=binding['text'], span_start=binding['span_start'], span_end=binding['span_end'],
423	            work_id=m.work_id, attribution=prose, card=c)
424	
425	    def admitted_ids(self) -> list[str]:
426	        return [cid for cid in self.cards if self.resolve(cid).ok]
427	
428	    def names_a_source(self, text: str) -> str:
429	        """The surname/venue check, used ONLY to REFUSE. Returns the offending token, or ''.
430	
431	        This is not `_cited_cards()` coming back in through a window. That function asked "WHICH source
432	        is this sentence about?" and answered with a guess, then gated the sentence against the guess.
433	        This one asks "does this text name ANY source at all?" and uses the answer only to say NO. There
434	        is no lane in which a surname selects a card.
435	
436	        It refuses on THREE grounds: (1) a corpus surname or single-word venue; (2) a venue name — a
437	        corpus multi-word venue, or a well-known journal/reporter the model typed from memory though the
438	        corpus never held it (Sol's "Science"); (3) a reporting-attribution construction ("<X> reports
439	        that ..."), which names a source for ANY invented venue, not only the ones we can enumerate.
